mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with declining unemployment indicates a tightening labor market, which raises corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of PAYEMS > 150k and UNRATE < 4.0% occurs but 5-day SPX returns are negative in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation pressures prompt the Fed to accelerate rate hikes, creating headwinds for equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined condition of CPIAUCSL > 295 and FEDFUNDS > 3.5% occurs but 60-day SPX returns are positive in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts signal robust consumer demand and business investment, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST exceeds 1400k but 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production indicates weak corporate output and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and creates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO falls below 104.0 but 5-day SPX returns are positive in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with weak GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a demand-side recession, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined condition of RSAFS < 680k and GDPC1 < 20000 occurs but 60-day SPX returns are positive in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE exceeds 4.0% but 20-day SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monetary policy rate hikes (FEDFUNDS) create higher discount rates for equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% but 5-day SPX returns are positive in > 50% of cases.
